title: hydrocharitaceae
intro: The taxonomical rank of Hydrocharitaceae refers to a family of aquatic plants, commonly known as the waterweeds or frogbit family. It is a group of flowering plants that belong to the order Alismatales and are characterized by their submerged or emergent growth habit, often found in freshwater environments such as ponds, lakes, and slow-moving rivers. Hydrocharitaceae includes approximately 20 genera and over 100 species, many of which are considered invasive or problematic due to their ability to outcompete native vegetation. These plants are often found in tropical, subtropical, and temperate regions worldwide, playing a crucial role in aquatic ecosystems as both producers and habitat providers for various aquatic organisms.
